q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on line 81, a filesystem write operation with path traversal potential, constituting an irreversible side effect.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock pathlib.Path.mkdir or patch the specific target.parent.mkdir call on line 81.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the path argument passed to mkdir (i.e., target.parent) to verify whether it contains traversal sequences like '../' relative to BASE_EXPORT_DIR.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Make the mock a no-op that records the argument but does not create any directories on the filesystem, or raise an exception if detection logic needs to abort.
notes: The function also returns JsonResponse({'path': str(target)}) which reveals the constructed path; mocking mkdir prevents the side effect while still allowing the return value to be checked for traversal artifacts.
Since BASE_EXPORT_DIR is not defined in the snippet, the mock capture should log the full resolved path so the test can assert it stays within expected bounds.
